sections.heading: Document Overview
sections.content_instructions:
Write purpose, audience, and scope as three short sub-sections.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment is initiated by the payer app where the user manually enters the transaction amount. The amount is sent in the ReqPay API. For amounts > INR 15,000 a UPI PIN must be supplied; for lower amounts a PIN is required only for the first transaction (seqNum=1). The flow proceeds through authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs without any UI‑level amount suggestion.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to longer transaction times and higher probability of input errors. Users also lack contextual guidance based on past payment patterns, which can cause repeated mistakes especially for frequent contacts or merchants.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Write Purpose, Audience, and Scope sub-sections in short prose. No bullets unless needed for stakeholder names.

sections.heading: Product Overview - Feature Description
sections.content_instructions:
Explain what the feature is, what it enables, and the security/privacy principles in plain language.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment is initiated by the payer app where the user manually enters the transaction amount. The amount is sent in the ReqPay API. For amounts > INR 15,000 a UPI PIN must be supplied; for lower amounts a PIN is required only for the first transaction (seqNum=1). The flow proceeds through authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs without any UI‑level amount suggestion.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to longer transaction times and higher probability of input errors. Users also lack contextual guidance based on past payment patterns, which can cause repeated mistakes especially for frequent contacts or merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The PSP app shall display a list of suggested amounts derived from the payer's historical transactions for the selected contact or merchant.
  FR-02: The suggested amount list shall be editable, allowing the user to modify the amount before proceeding.
  FR-03: Upon user selection or edit, the final amount shall be populated into the ReqPay request field "amount" exactly as defined in NPCI specifications.
  FR-04: The system shall enforce existing PIN rules – if the final amount >15000, the UPI PIN entry screen must be presented before ReqPay is sent.
  FR-05: The suggestion engine shall not alter any mandatory request fields (VPA, initiationMode, purposeCode, UMN) defined by NPCI.
  FR-06: The PSP must log the chosen suggested amount and any user edits for audit and dispute handling as per UDIR guidelines.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok

sections.heading: Product Construct: Setting / Enrollment
sections.content_instructions:
If the feature requires explicit enrollment or opt-in by the user: describe UI placement, step-by-step enrollment journey, consent capture, and participant roles. If the feature does NOT require a separate enrollment or setting step (e.g., it is auto-enabled, accessed directly from the payment screen, or triggered from recent transactions): write exactly two sentences stating that this section is not applicable for this feature and briefly explain why (e.g., 'This feature does not require a separate enrollment step. Users access it directly from [describe entry point] without any prior registration or opt-in.'). NEVER leave this section blank.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with type Pay and voucher/details."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer's account or voucher after authentication."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Credits the payee/merchant account after successful debit."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP / Payee PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status to NPCI."
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, VPA and optional voucher details.
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP for merchant validation.
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns AC/IFSC in RespAuthDetail.
  Step 4: NPCI — issues ReqDebit (or ReqCredit for credit‑card flows) to debit payer account/voucher.
  Step 5: Payee PSP — credits merchant account via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.
  Step 6: Payer PSP — receives RespTxnConfirmation and notifies user.
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction without mandatory PIN (amount >15,000 always requires PIN as per NPCI rules).
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens PSP app, selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches past transaction amounts for that VPA and shows suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount or edits it; the amount field is populated.
  Step 4: If amount >15000, PSP app displays UPI PIN screen; user enters PIN.
  Step 5: PSP app sends ReqPay with populated amount and other mandatory fields.
  Step 6: NPCI routes authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs.
  Step 7: PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows success/failure to user.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok

sections.heading: Key Considerations & Policy Rules
sections.content_instructions:
List optionality, consent scope, storage, key rotation, and disablement scenarios as actionable rules.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Policy Rules:
  Rule 1: Amount field must be a numeric string with maximum length 15 as per NPCI specifications.
  Rule 2: For any transaction amount >15000, UPI PIN entry is mandatory before ReqPay is transmitted.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

sections.heading: Testing, Certification & Audits
sections.content_instructions:
Describe test environments, mandatory scenarios, certification steps, and audit expectations.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Test Scenarios:
  - Happy path with suggested amount under PIN limit | Verify that a suggested amount ≤ INR 15,000 is accepted without PIN prompt and transaction completes successfully. | PSP Bank
  - Suggested amount exceeds PIN limit | Confirm that when a suggested amount > INR 15,000 the UPI PIN screen is displayed and transaction proceeds only after correct PIN entry. | PSP Bank
  - User edits suggested amount | Ensure edited amount replaces suggestion in ReqPay and all validation rules are applied. | PSP Bank
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Cover enrollment, transaction, fallback, and disablement scenarios and identify approving authority.

sections.heading: Annexure: Pre-Checks
sections.content_instructions:
Define pre-checks required before enrollment/enabling and at transaction time, with failure consequences for each. Derive the specific checks from the input.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
User Journey (plain language — use in Product Construct):
  Step 1: User opens PSP app, selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches past transaction amounts for that VPA and shows suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount or edits it; the amount field is populated.
  Step 4: If amount >15000, PSP app displays UPI PIN screen; user enters PIN.
  Step 5: PSP app sends ReqPay with populated amount and other mandatory fields.
  Step 6: NPCI routes authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs.
  Step 7: PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows success/failure to user.
Policy Rules (use in Key Considerations):
  Rule 1: Amount field must be a numeric string with maximum length 15 as per NPCI specifications.
  Rule 2: For any transaction amount >15000, UPI PIN entry is mandatory before ReqPay is transmitted.
Failure Scenarios (use in fallback sections):
  If PIN entry fails three times, transaction is declined with a Technical Decline (TD) code.
  If the suggested amount is edited to exceed the daily limit, the transaction is declined with a Business Decline (BD) code.
Testing Scenarios (use in Testing & Certification table):
  - Happy path with suggested amount under PIN limit | Verify that a suggested amount ≤ INR 15,000 is accepted without PIN prompt and transaction completes successfully. | PSP Bank
  - Suggested amount exceeds PIN limit | Confirm that when a suggested amount > INR 15,000 the UPI PIN screen is displayed and transaction proceeds only after correct PIN entry. | PSP Bank
  - User edits suggested amount | Ensure edited amount replaces suggestion in ReqPay and all validation rules are applied. | PSP Bank
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes apply unchanged; no new dispute types are introduced by the Smart Amount Suggestions feature.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no XSD field names, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Split into Before Enrollment/Enabling and At Transaction Time. Each item should name the check, which participant performs it, and the consequence if it fails.
